subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the importance of water in daily life
Learn about sources of water like rivers, lakes, and groundwater
Know about water scarcity and reasons behind it
Learn simple methods of conserving water
Develop observation and problem-solving skills related to water

lesson_plan.title: Importance of Water
topics_covered: Why water is essential for life
Uses of water in households, agriculture, and industry
teaching_strategy: Ask students to list how they use water daily
student_activities: List all uses of water they can think of
Share stories or examples from home about water use

lesson_plan.title: Sources of Water
teaching_strategy: Show pictures of rivers, lakes, and wells
Ask students to identify local water sources
student_activities: Draw a diagram of a river, lake, and well
List sources of water in their locality

lesson_plan.title: Water Cycle
topics_covered: Runoff and collection in rivers and lakes
teaching_strategy: Demonstrate a simple water cycle using a bowl and plastic wrap
Use charts and diagrams to explain
student_activities: Explain the stages in their own words

lesson_plan.title: Water Scarcity and Causes
topics_covered: Overuse, pollution, uneven distribution, and drought
teaching_strategy: Show pictures of dry rivers and wells
Ask students to suggest reasons for shortage
student_activities: Discuss what happens when water is wasted

lesson_plan.title: Water Conservation Methods
topics_covered: Saving water at home and school
Planting trees and protecting lakes and rivers
teaching_strategy: Show videos or pictures of water-saving methods
Demonstrate simple ways to save water in daily life
student_activities: Draw a simple rainwater harvesting system
List three ways they can save water daily

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Fun Quiz
topics_covered: Recap of water uses, sources, cycle, scarcity, and conservation
Relate lessons to students’ own water use
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with pictures and multiple choice
Class discussion about personal water habits
student_activities: Draw or share one thing they learned about saving water

teaching_aids: Pictures of rivers, lakes, wells, and water cycles
Simple models or diagrams for demonstration
teacher_tip: Make lessons interactive and visual. Use stories, drawings, and charts. Encourage students to share experiences about water at home and school.
